title: [circt-synth] A LEC failure in FmaaSgn benchmark
body:
```mlir
  hw.module @FmaaSgn20(in %a : i20, in %b : i20, in %c : i61, in %d : i61, out res : i61) {
    // Sign-extend a (i20 -> i61)
    %0 = comb.extract %a from 19 : (i20) -> i1
    %1 = comb.replicate %0 : (i1) -> i41
    %2 = comb.concat %1, %a : i41, i20
    // Sign-extend b (i20 -> i61)
    %3 = comb.extract %b from 19 : (i20) -> i1
    %4 = comb.replicate %3 : (i1) -> i41
    %5 = comb.concat %4, %b : i41, i20
    %6 = comb.mul %2, %5 : i61
    %7 = comb.sub %6, %c : i61
    %8 = comb.sub %7, %d : i61
    hw.output %8 : i61
  }
```

```bash
$ circt-synth test.mlir -o after.mlir
$ circt-lec test.mlir after.mlir --c1 FmaaSgn20 --c2 FmaaSgn20
c1 != c2
```

ABC's cec says it fails when all inputs are 0.  
```mlir

module {
  hw.module @FmaaSgn20(in %a : i20, in %b : i20, in %c : i61, in %d : i61, out res : i61) {
    // Sign-extend a (i20 -> i61)
    %0 = comb.extract %a from 19 : (i20) -> i1
    %1 = comb.replicate %0 : (i1) -> i41
    %2 = comb.concat %1, %a : i41, i20
    // Sign-extend b (i20 -> i61)
    %3 = comb.extract %b from 19 : (i20) -> i1
    %4 = comb.replicate %3 : (i1) -> i41
    %5 = comb.concat %4, %b : i41, i20
    %6 = comb.mul %2, %5 : i61
    %7 = comb.sub %6, %c : i61
    %8 = comb.sub %7, %d : i61
    hw.output %8 : i61
  }

  // Simulate @FmaaSgn20 with all-zero inputs and emit the result.
  // Expected: res = 0*0 - 0 - 0 = 0
  func.func @main() {
    %a = arith.constant 0 : i20
    %b = arith.constant 0 : i20
    %c = arith.constant 0 : i61
    %d = arith.constant 0 : i61

    arc.sim.instantiate @FmaaSgn20 as %model {
      arc.sim.set_input %model, "a" = %a : i20, !arc.sim.instance<@FmaaSgn20>
      arc.sim.set_input %model, "b" = %b : i20, !arc.sim.instance<@FmaaSgn20>
      arc.sim.set_input %model, "c" = %c : i61, !arc.sim.instance<@FmaaSgn20>
      arc.sim.set_input %model, "d" = %d : i61, !arc.sim.instance<@FmaaSgn20>
      arc.sim.step %model : !arc.sim.instance<@FmaaSgn20>

      %res = arc.sim.get_port %model, "res" : i61, !arc.sim.instance<@FmaaSgn20>
      arc.sim.emit "res", %res : i61
      %res_msb = comb.extract %res from 58 : (i61) -> i1
      arc.sim.emit "res[58]", %res_msb : i1
    }
    return
  }
}

```
```
$ arcilator test.mlir --run --jit-entry=main
res = 0
res[58] = 0
$ <run `circt-synth -convert-to-comb` and manually swap FmaaSgn20>
$ arcilator test.mlir --run --jit-entry=main
res = 400000000000000
res[58] = 1
```

FYI @cowardsa
